questions: Should I stretch before or after workout?
answers: Stretching improves flexibility, reduces muscle tension, increases range of motion, and helps prevent injuries. Do dynamic stretches before exercise and static stretches after to maximize benefits.

For beginners, try hamstring stretches, quad stretches, shoulder rolls, neck tilts, and cat-cow stretches. Hold static stretches for 15-30 seconds and never bounce. Stretch to mild discomfort, not pain.

Stretching holo muscle ar joint ke flexible rakhbar exercise. Workout er age dynamic stretching koro (moving stretches) ar pore static stretching koro (hold kore rakho). Daily stretching korle body flexible thake.

Consistent daily stretching for even 10-15 minutes can significantly improve flexibility and reduce pain. Focus on major muscle groups: hamstrings, hip flexors, shoulders, and back. Morning stretching can energize you, while evening stretching helps relaxation.
